datasetInfo.title: Ökofalvak, szándékközösségek és öko-hubok + changemaker események
datasetInfo.description: Ökofalvak, szándékos életközösségek és öko-hubok kurált hálózata (Magyarország-első + nemzetközi GEN-tagok), valamint a Q3/Q4 2026 és 2027 közötti, changemakerek számára releváns események.

initiatives.name: Galgafarm / Galgahévíz Ökofalu
initiatives.description: An organic farming and intentional community in the Galga valley north of Budapest focused on biodynamic agriculture, community resilience, and ecological education.

initiatives.name: MAG / Magfalva Community
initiatives.description: A Hungarian intentional community and seed-saving network focused on seed sovereignty, permaculture, and sustainable rural living.

initiatives.name: Gömörszőlős Ökofalu
initiatives.description: A sustainable-village development led by the Ecological Institute (Ökológiai Intézet) in a small northern Hungarian village, combining renewable energy, traditional crafts, and environmental education.

initiatives.name: Agostyán / Ágoston-liget Ökofalu
initiatives.description: A pioneer Hungarian ecovillage tied to the Natural Lifestyle Foundation (Természetes Életmód Alapítvány) and the Ágoston-liget arboretum, focused on natural building, organic gardening, and nature-based living near Tata.

initiatives.name: Nagyszékely
initiatives.description: A depopulated Tolna village revitalized by an in-migrant community of homesteaders, permaculturists, and artists pursuing self-sufficiency and back-to-the-land living.

initiatives.name: Cserdi Közösségi Program
initiatives.description: A Roma village-renewal and social-regeneration project in Baranya built on community agriculture, self-sufficiency, and empowerment, internationally recognized as a rural regeneration model.

initiatives.name: Rozsály Önfenntartó Falu
initiatives.description: A self-sustaining village regeneration model in Szabolcs-Szatmár-Bereg built on local food production and a public-work-based local economy.

initiatives.name: Mandala Ökofalu
initiatives.description: A permaculture-focused ecovillage and learning community in Somogy county practicing natural building and regenerative land use.

initiatives.name: Nyim Ökoközösség
initiatives.description: A small intentional eco-community in a Somogy village centered on a community house, permaculture gardening, and sustainable rural living.

initiatives.name: Findhorn Foundation
initiatives.description: One of the world's most celebrated spiritual ecovillages, founded in 1962 on the Moray coast of Scotland, blending spiritual practice with ecological living and education. A GEN flagship and UN-recognized model of sustainability.

initiatives.name: Tamera
initiatives.description: A peace research village and healing biotope in southern Portugal developing models for sustainable coexistence, water retention landscapes, and nonviolent conflict resolution.

initiatives.name: Ökodorf Sieben Linden
initiatives.description: A well-established German ecovillage of around 150 residents practicing low-impact living with straw-bale construction, permaculture, and consensus governance. A flagship of GEN Europe.

initiatives.name: Damanhur
initiatives.description: A spiritual eco-society and federation of communities in the Italian Alpine foothills, renowned for its hand-carved underground Temples of Humankind and its blend of art, spirituality, and sustainable living.

initiatives.name: Auroville
initiatives.description: An international township of around 3,500 residents from 60+ nations near Pondicherry, dedicated to human unity and conscious living per the vision of Sri Aurobindo and The Mother. A UNESCO-supported model for sustainable development.

initiatives.name: Schloss Tempelhof
initiatives.description: A fast-growing ecovillage community of 150+ adults and children on a historic castle estate in southern Germany, practicing permaculture, nonviolent communication, and sociocracy.

initiatives.name: ZEGG
initiatives.description: The Centre for Experimental Cultural Design near Berlin, a pioneering community known for developing the ZEGG Forum method and integrating ecology, community, and conscious relationships.

initiatives.name: Longo Maï
initiatives.description: A network of self-managed cooperative farming communities spanning France, Switzerland, Austria, and beyond, founded after May 1968 to build rural autonomy through organic farming, crafts, and political solidarity.

initiatives.name: Lakabe
initiatives.description: One of Spain's oldest and most influential ecovillages, an abandoned medieval hamlet in the Navarrese Pyrenees revived in 1980 and known for self-sufficiency, renewable energy, and communal economy.

initiatives.name: Lebensgarten Steyerberg
initiatives.description: A spiritual-ecological community in a former munitions-factory complex in Lower Saxony, one of Germany's first ecovillages and a founding member of GEN, integrating holistic health and ecological building.

initiatives.name: Kibbutz Lotan
initiatives.description: An ecological kibbutz of the Reform Jewish movement in the Arava Desert, internationally known for its permaculture centre and mud-brick eco-campus. A GEN member and frequent host of ecovillage gatherings.

initiatives.name: Torri Superiore
initiatives.description: A restored medieval hamlet in the Maritime Alps near the French-Italian border housing an ecovillage focused on ecological restoration, permaculture, and intercultural exchange. A GEN Europe member.

initiatives.name: Crystal Waters
initiatives.description: The world's first permaculture-designed ecovillage, in the Sunshine Coast hinterland of Queensland, a UN-awarded landmark in intentional community and sustainable land-use planning.

events.name: European Ecovillage Gathering 2026 — Dare to Trust
events.description: Europe's largest annual ecovillage festival, hosted by Nature Community in Upper Palatinate Bavaria, gathering 800+ people from 40+ countries under the theme 'Dare to Trust'. Organised by GEN Europe.

events.name: Caux Inner Development Goals (IDG) Forum 2026
events.description: A five-day immersive gathering at the historic Caux Palace in the Swiss Alps exploring inner development and systemic-change leadership through contemplative practice. Co-organised by Initiatives of Change and the IDG network.

events.name: Transition Design Conference 2026 — Weaving Regenerative Futures
events.description: The 5th International Transition Design Conference, co-organised by TEC de Monterrey, University of Palermo and Carnegie Mellon, bringing together academics and practitioners in systems-change, design and regenerative futures.

events.name: Tamera Summer Gathering 2026
events.description: Annual summer gathering at Tamera Healing Biotope, a peace research and education centre in southern Portugal working on ecological restoration, community living and a new planetary culture.

events.name: Findhorn Musical Retreat — Sound, Community & Healing
events.description: A seven-day residential retreat at Ecovillage Findhorn blending piano concerts, community singing, sacred dance and shared stillness, cultivating connection and healing through music in an intentional-community setting.

events.name: Findhorn Permaculture Design Certificate (PDC) 2026
events.description: A two-week hands-on Permaculture Design Certificate course at one of Europe's oldest and largest ecovillages, facilitated by Gaia Education certified instructors with a head/heart/hands approach.

events.name: Work That Reconnects Retreat — Bekkola gård, Norway
events.description: A five-day co-creative retreat spiraling through Joanna Macy's Work That Reconnects at Bekkola gård in Finnskogen forest, bringing together practitioners in deep ecology, grief work and systemic healing.

events.name: Gyüttment Találkozó 2026
events.description: Hungary's annual gathering of ecological lifestyle changers and rural re-settlers at the historic Csobánkapuszta estate — workshops, nature-based-living knowledge sharing, crafts marketplace and community connection; a zero-waste event.

events.name: IST 2026 — International Sustainability Transitions Conference
events.description: The flagship annual academic conference of the Sustainability Transitions Research Network (STRN), drawing 500+ researchers from 30+ countries on socio-technical transformation across energy, food, mobility and social-innovation systems.

events.name: EuPN Permaculture Gathering — Afrinoon, Brittany
events.description: A self-organising, open-agenda weekend gathering of European permaculture practitioners at Afrinoon Permaculture farm (a registered EuPN Permahub), limited to ~38 participants for deep peer-to-peer learning and network weaving.

events.name: Work That Reconnects Retreat at Findhorn
events.description: A multi-week residential retreat at Ecovillage Findhorn drawing on Joanna Macy's Work That Reconnects methodology — deep ecology, systems thinking and collective ritual cultivating capacity to engage in the healing of the world.

events.name: IDG Business Forum 2026
events.description: A two-day in-person convening for business leaders, changemakers and entrepreneurs organised by the Inner Development Goals initiative, focused on embedding inner development into organisations and leadership to drive systemic change.

events.name: ECTN International Conference for Cultural & Sustainable Tourism 2026
events.description: The 19th annual ECTN conference on regenerative, smart and resilient cultural tourism, culminating in the Destination of Sustainable Cultural Tourism Awards.

events.name: SOCAP26 — Social Capital Markets Conference
events.description: The world's largest impact-investing and social-enterprise convening, bringing together investors, foundation leaders, impact entrepreneurs and systems-change practitioners to unlock capital for climate, justice and regenerative futures.

events.name: SOCIN 2026 — Scientific Conference on Social Innovation
events.description: Academic and practitioner conference at SWPS University co-organised by the ERUA network, with streams on systemic change, social imaginaries, technology for transformation and impact-oriented organisations.

events.name: CSRF 2026 — Conference on Sustainable and Regenerative Farming
events.description: The 3rd international conference on sustainable and regenerative farming, exploring research and practice across soil health, agroecology and food-system transition; onsite and online participation.

events.name: IPC16 — 16th International Permaculture Convergence: Beyond Sustainability
events.description: The flagship global permaculture gathering held every two years: a 10-day event spanning a conference near Bangkok and a convergence in Korat, under the theme 'Beyond Sustainability — Many Paths'.
events.location: Pathum Thani / Wang Nam Khiao, Thailand

events.name: European Ecovillage Gathering 2027
events.description: The annual GEN Europe ecovillage gathering; host location for 2027 is under open call as of mid-2026. Dates estimated from the established mid-July pattern of prior editions.

events.name: IPC17 — 17th International Permaculture Convergence (2027)
events.description: The biennial global permaculture convergence following IPC16 Thailand (2026); host nation not yet announced. Dates estimated from the biennial cadence and typical autumn timing of previous editions.

events.name: SOCIN 2027 — Scientific Conference on Social Innovation
events.description: The 2027 edition of the biennial academic-practitioner conference on social innovation and systemic change co-organised by the ERUA university network; theme, location and exact dates estimated from prior editions.
